Bus Timings

Abu Dhabi 380 Bus Timings from Al Ain to Al Faqa (Al Rahman Mosque)

August 15, 2025
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

Abu Dhabi’s Bus 380 is a convenient transportation route connecting Al Ain Bus Station to Al Faqa, ending at the Al Rahman Mosque. Whether you’re heading for work, visiting a mosque, or exploring the city’s outskirts, Bus 380 provides a comfortable and reliable travel option.

Operational Details

Bus 380 operates four times a day, both from Al Ain and Al Faqa. Below are the timings for Bus 380:

From Al Ain From Al Faqa (Al Rahman Mosque)
06:00 08:00
10:00 12:00
14:00 16:00
18:00 20:00

Bus 380 Timetable

To view the full schedule, you can visit the official AD Mobility website or follow this link for the 380 Bus Timings.

Ticket Pricing: Payments for Bus 380 are made using the Hafilat Card, Abu Dhabi’s cashless payment system. This card ensures seamless travel across all buses. The base fare is AED 2, with additional charges depending on the distance traveled. Passengers can top up their Hafilat cards online or at kiosks located throughout the city.

Route Overview

Below is a detailed list of major stops along the route:

  1. Al Ain Bus Station The starting point of the journey, centrally located in Al Ain.

  2. Department of Public Health A stop near the city’s health services hub.

3.

  1. Imam Shafi’i Mosque A notable mosque in the area.

  2. Al Ain Hospital A key healthcare facility for locals and visitors.

  3. Al Ain Municipality East A government office offering public services.

  4. Al Jimi Mall A popular shopping center with a variety of retail, dining, and entertainment options.

  5. Hazza bin Zayed Stadium A major sports venue in the city.

  6. Ain Al Khaleej Hospital Another important healthcare stop along the route.

  7. Al Muqqar Street A residential street with several local amenities.

  8. Al Jimi Police Station A stop near law enforcement offices.

  9. Safeer Mall A smaller mall catering to local shoppers.

  10. Equestrian Club A stop near the Al Ain Equestrian Club, offering leisure activities.

  11. Al Misbah Mosque Another key mosque serving the community.

  12. Al Hareth Bin Hisham Mosque A religious center for the area’s residents.

  13. Hili Fun City A popular amusement park, ideal for families and tourists.

  14. Al Foaa Police School A facility for police training.

  15. Al Foa’a Police Station Providing safety and security for the community.

  16. Al Foa’a Company A commercial area with businesses and offices.

  17. ADNOC A major stop offering refueling services.

  18. Farm Various rural stops offering glimpses of the agricultural landscape.

  19. Aqar Agricultural Company A farming company that highlights the region’s agricultural significance.

Recommended Guides

Enhance your daily commute with these essential transport guides:

Complete RTA Intercity Routes Guide Largest Bus Stations Infrastructure in UAE

  1. Water Tanks Stops servicing rural and farm areas.

  2. Al Hayer Police Station A law enforcement stop serving the outskirts.

  3. Al Hayer Emirates Post A postal service stop for Al Hayer.

26.

  1. Hamad Mutawa Al Ketbi Mosque Another significant religious landmark.

  2. Rest Area A place for travelers to take a break during the journey.

  3. Al Shuwaib Bridge A key crossing connecting different parts of the region.

  4. Al Faqa Petrol Station A stop for refueling near Al Faqa.

  5. Sheikh Hamad Bin Tahnoon Mosque A notable mosque in the Al Faqa area.

  6. Faqa, Terminus / Al Faqa Family Park A family-friendly park offering a place to relax.

  7. Souq A local market where passengers can shop.

  8. Slaughter House A service stop within the rural area.

  9. Al Rahman Mosque The final stop in Al Faqa, serving as a religious and community center.

Accessibility and Services

The buses feature:

  • Ramps for wheelchair access.

  • Designated seating for individuals requiring assistance.
  • Audio-visual aids to assist passengers with hearing or vision impairments.

This makes Bus 380 an inclusive choice for all commuters.

Nearby Attractions and Landmarks

  1. Hili Fun City One of Al Ain’s oldest amusement parks, offering fun rides for families and tourists.

  2. Hazza bin Zayed Stadium A state-of-the-art sports complex where football matches and events are regularly held.

  3. Al Jimi Mall A premier shopping destination in Al Ain, housing international brands and diverse dining options.

These attractions make the journey on Bus 380 not just a commute but an opportunity to explore Al Ain’s vibrant culture and recreational facilities.

Journey Experience

On a recent trip on Bus 380 from Al Ain to Al Faqa, travelers enjoyed a scenic blend of urban and rural landscapes. Along the way, passengers caught glimpses of Hili Fun City, an amusement park perfect for families.

The route meandered through peaceful agricultural areas, showcasing Al Ain’s rich farming heritage. Finally, the bus stopped at Al Rahman Mosque in Al Faqa, a serene community centerpiece. Overall, the journey provided a unique opportunity to explore the culture and attractions of Al Ain. It’s a must-try for anyone looking to experience the local charm.

Travel Tips for Bus 380

  • Plan Ahead: With only four trips a day, make sure to check the bus schedule to avoid long waits.

  • Use the Hafilat Card: This makes fare payments quick and hassle-free, ensuring smooth boarding.
  • Monitor Bus Updates: Stay informed about real-time changes in the bus schedule by using the AD Mobility app.
  • Explore Attractions: Take time to visit nearby landmarks like Hili Fun City or Al Jimi Mall to make the most of your trip.

Whether you’re commuting for work, visiting family, or exploring local attractions, Bus 380 offers an affordable and accessible travel option for all. With its convenient schedules and connections to significant destinations, this bus route plays a vital role in Abu Dhabi’s public transportation system. Be sure to check out the complete bus routes available in Abu Dhabi!

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)

Editorial review date: August 15, 2025.
